analysis: The stretch opened rough — 14 July was a scrappy 5h 57m — but you bounced back hard with a 9h 22m recovery night that included over two hours of REM, which is genuinely gorgeous. The middle of the week held solid, with four nights sitting between 7h 15m and 7h 55m, and that 53-minute deep sleep on the 17th is the kind of thing your body really thanks you for. The back end is where it gets a bit spicy: the last two nights crept up to 51 minutes and then 1h 22m of awake time, which suggests something's disrupting you in the early morning hours even when you're getting decent overall totals. All your wind-down rituals are clearly doing something — the consistency across that middle chunk is real — but those late-stretch wake windows are worth watching.
